headline: NexGen Energy Gets FEDERAL GREEN LIGHT For Rook I — Summer Construction Start, 30M lb/yr Uranium Output On Track
snippet: NexGen Energy (TSX/NYSE: NXE) received final federal approval for its Rook I uranium project in Saskatchewan on March 5, 2026, and will begin construction this summer. The Arrow deposit will produce ~30 million lb of U3O8/yr — surpassing Cameco's Cigar Lake — over an 11-year mine life.

headline: TRUMP SETS JULY 13 DEADLINE On Critical Minerals — Section 232 Negotiations Must Show Progress or Tariffs Follow
snippet: Trump's Proclamation 11001 found critical mineral imports threaten US national security but chose negotiations over immediate tariffs. The July 13, 2026 reporting deadline is now 93 days away — failing which the administration may impose minimum import prices or blanket import restrictions.

headline: Goldman Sachs Sets $4,900 GOLD TARGET By Year-End — Names Copper Its Top Long-Term Industrial Metal
snippet: Goldman Sachs forecasts gold rising to $4,900/oz by December 2026, driven by sustained central bank buying and safe-haven demand. The bank simultaneously named copper its top long-term industrial metal pick, despite projecting near-term weakness tied to tariff risk and a 160kt supply surplus.

headline: Coeur Mining CLOSES New Gold Deal — Seven-Asset North American Producer Targets $3B EBITDA, $2B Free Cash Flow In 2026
snippet: Coeur Mining completed its acquisition of New Gold on March 19, 2026, combining seven North American operations into a new senior precious metals producer. The deal adds New Afton and Rainy River, lifting Coeur's gold production by 80% and targeting $3B EBITDA and $2B free cash flow for 2026.

headline: Goldman Sachs: COPPER CORRECTION Due — Record $14,500/t Too High, $11,000 Target On 25% Tariff Risk and China Demand Slump
snippet: Goldman Sachs is warning copper's January 2026 record of $14,500/tonne won't hold, citing a 160kt supply surplus, Chinese demand down 8% year-over-year in Q4 2025, and an expected 25% US copper tariff by June. The bank targets LME copper at $10,710 for H1 2026.

headline: NexGold Intersects 1.79 g/t Au Over 25.6 Metres at Goldlund Deposit, Ontario — Zone 4 INFILL Expands Footprint
snippet: NexGold Mining Corp. (TSX-V: NEXG) drilled 1.79 g/t Au over 25.6m and 2.31 g/t Au over 21.5m in Zone 4 of the Goldlund Deposit at the Goliath Gold Complex, Ontario. Results are part of an ongoing infill-and-expansion program targeting a resource update at the advanced-stage project.

headline: Marimaca Copper ADDS Silver Dimension at Pampa Medina — TSX/ASX Dual-Lister Reports Consolidated High-Grade Assays
snippet: Marimaca Copper (TSX: MARI; ASX: MC2) reported consolidated high-grade silver assays from drilling at its Pampa Medina deposit in Chile, adding a meaningful silver dimension to what has been a copper-focused story. The results expand the multi-metal potential of the project.

headline: 837 g/t SILVER HIT Expands Argentina Gold-Silver System — Feeder Zones Begin To Converge At Depth
snippet: A 837 g/t silver intercept has materially expanded an emerging gold-silver system in Argentina, with feeder zones from multiple directions beginning to converge at depth. The result points to a potentially significant undiscovered high-grade system in a well-established mineralized district.

headline: Saskatchewan: NexGen + Denison Uranium Mines To Create 1,600 CONSTRUCTION JOBS, $2.8B Investment — Province Calls It Generational
snippet: The Saskatchewan government confirmed the NexGen Rook I and Denison Wheeler River uranium projects will together generate 1,600+ construction jobs, 700+ operational roles, and a $2.8B combined investment — calling the projects a generational economic opportunity for northern Saskatchewan.

headline: Argus: US Copper SUPPLY CHAIN At Risk — 25% Tariff Expected By June 2026 Threatens Downstream Manufacturers
snippet: Argus Media's viewpoint flags serious uncertainty for US copper consumers ahead of an expected 25% import tariff on refined copper by June 2026. Domestic smelter capacity is insufficient to replace imports short-term, putting downstream manufacturers and project developers in a difficult position.

headline: Bain: Mining Must GET SERIOUS About M&A — Record Margins and Disruptive Themes Open Rare Consolidation Window
snippet: A new Bain & Company mining M&A report argues executives are still under-leveraging strong balance sheets and record commodity prices to consolidate. The report singles out AI energy demand, defence minerals, and battery transition as the disruptive themes any acquirer must align with to create durable value.

headline: Lithium, Cobalt, Nickel DIVERGE Sharply — Credendo Flags Volatile Cycle Fragmentation Across Battery Metal Complex
snippet: A Credendo market update finds lithium, cobalt, nickel, and copper are experiencing dramatically different supply-demand dynamics in 2026. Lithium faces the most complex outlook as Chinese supply dominance, EV adoption curves, and surging stationary storage demand pull prices in competing directions.
